title: Cronos Group Reports 2020 Fourth Quarter and Full-Year Results
text: Cronos Group reported 2020 full-year net revenue of $46.7 million, up 97% from $23.8 million in 2019. However, company faced significant challenges with gross loss of $25.8 million compared to $17.6 million loss in 2019. Net loss reached $75.3 million versus net income of $1.17 billion in 2019. Adjusted EBITDA loss widened to $147.3 million from $98.3 million loss in 2019. The company maintained approximately $1.3 billion in cash and short-term investments, providing financial cushion despite operational losses. Growth came from expanded Canadian adult-use sales, U.S. segment expansion through full year of Redwood acquisition, and Israeli medical market entry. Significant inventory write-downs of $26.1 million due to Canadian cannabis price compression dampened results. Q4 2020 net revenue was $17.0 million, up 133% year-over-year with gross loss of $14.9 million improving from $20.1 million loss. Q4 net loss was $111.7 million with inventory write-down of $15.0 million. Happy Dance CBD products secured placement in over 550 ULTA Beauty locations. Cronos Israel obtained cultivation licenses and established distribution partnerships.
